revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"
Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23388260132|commit triggered pipeline]]

[[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Disputed%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} __NOTOC__ {{For|the inline template|Template:Disputed inline}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} {{nosubst}} This template identifies a Wikipedia article as having content whose truth or factual nature is in dispute. If the problem is confined to one section, or a few sections in an article that has several, it is better to use {{tlg|Disputed section}}. * This [[Wikipedia:Template namespace|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. == Usage == First add a new section named "Disputed" to the article's [[Help:Using talk pages|talk page]], describing the problems with the disputed statements. Then place {{Disputed}} at the top of the disputed article. If the talk page discussion is not in a section named "Disputed", use {{Tlx|Disputed|''talk page section name''}} (for a talk page section named "Disputed information", use {{Tlx|Disputed|Disputed information}} in the article). If there is no talk page, the tag won't refer to it (in which case it's recommended to consider whether the tag should be there, since there is no reason given). Articles using this template as shown above are placed into [[:Category:Accuracy disputes]]. If a dispute spans multiple pages, please centralize the discussion in one location and link to it by specifying the talk page where the discussion is: {{tlx|Disputed|talkpage{{=}}Talk:Page name|section name|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}. This template can take an optional date parameter in the format {{Tlx|Disputed|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} or {{Tlx|Disputed|talk page section name|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}. If the date is not included it will be added later by a [[WP:BOT]]. Articles using this template with a date are added to a monthly subcategory of [[:Category:Accuracy disputes]]. ;Example : {{Tlx|Disputed|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} Output: {{Disputed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} Category: : [[:Category:Accuracy disputes from {{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}]] === Sections=== If applied to part of an article, this template takes an optional parameter "what", for example: {{tlx|Disputed|what{{=}}section}}. An alternative is to use either {{tl|Disputed section}} or {{tl|Dispute about}}. To make the message box small, set the "small" parameter to "left": :{{Tlx|Disputed|what{{=}}table|small{{=}}left}} Output: {{Disputed|what=table|small=left}} == Template Data == {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "params": { "what": { "description": "Type of content. Fits the sentence \"This (what) is disputed\"", "example": "section", "default": "article", "type": "line" }, "discuss": { "label": "1", "aliases": [ "talk", "talkpage" ], "description": "The location (Talk:Page, Section, or Talk:Page#Section) where the dispute is discussed.", "type": "wiki-page-name" }, "date": { "description": "Date template was added.", "example": "May 2016", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true } }, "description": "Identifies that the article has content whose truth or factual nature is in dispute." } == Redirects == *{{Tl|Accuracy}} *{{Tl|Accuracy dispute}} *{{Tl|BLP dispute}} *{{Tl|Content disputed}} *{{Tl|Factual accuracy}} *{{Tl|Factual accuracy disputed}} == See also == {{Dispute templates}} {{Citation and verifiability article maintenance templates}} {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Dispute templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24926181992|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} __NOTOC__ {{For|the inline template|Template:Disputed inline}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} {{nosubst}} This template identifies a Wikipedia article as having content whose truth or factual nature is in dispute. If the problem is confined to one section, or a few sections in an article that has several, it is better to use {{tlg|Disputed section}}. * This [[Wikipedia:Template namespace|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. == Usage == First add a new section named "Disputed" to the article's [[Help:Using talk pages|talk page]], describing the problems with the disputed statements. Then place {{Disputed}} at the top of the disputed article. If the talk page discussion is not in a section named "Disputed", use {{Tlx|Disputed|''talk page section name''}} (for a talk page section named "Disputed information", use {{Tlx|Disputed|Disputed information}} in the article). If there is no talk page, the tag won't refer to it (in which case it's recommended to consider whether the tag should be there, since there is no reason given). Articles using this template as shown above are placed into [[:Category:Accuracy disputes]]. If a dispute spans multiple pages, please centralize the discussion in one location and link to it by specifying the talk page where the discussion is: {{tlx|Disputed|talkpage{{=}}Talk:Page name|section name|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}. This template can take an optional date parameter in the format {{Tlx|Disputed|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} or {{Tlx|Disputed|talk page section name|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}. If the date is not included it will be added later by a [[WP:BOT]]. Articles using this template with a date are added to a monthly subcategory of [[:Category:Accuracy disputes]]. ;Example : {{Tlx|Disputed|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} Output: {{Disputed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} Category: : [[:Category:Accuracy disputes from {{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}]] === Sections=== If applied to part of an article, this template takes an optional parameter "what", for example: {{tlx|Disputed|what{{=}}section}}. An alternative is to use either {{tl|Disputed section}} or {{tl|Dispute about}}. To make the message box small, set the "small" parameter to "left": :{{Tlx|Disputed|what{{=}}table|small{{=}}left}} Output: {{Disputed|what=table|small=left}} == Template Data == {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "params": { "what": { "description": "Type of content. Fits the sentence \"This (what) is disputed\"", "example": "section", "default": "article", "type": "line" }, "discuss": { "label": "1", "aliases": [ "talk", "talkpage" ], "description": "The location (Talk:Page, Section, or Talk:Page#Section) where the dispute is discussed.", "type": "wiki-page-name" }, "date": { "description": "Date template was added.", "example": "May 2016", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true } }, "description": "Identifies that the article has content whose truth or factual nature is in dispute." } == Redirects == *{{Tl|Accuracy}} *{{Tl|Accuracy dispute}} *{{Tl|BLP dispute}} *{{Tl|Content disputed}} *{{Tl|Factual accuracy}} *{{Tl|Factual accuracy disputed}} == See also == {{Dispute templates}} {{Citation and verifiability article maintenance templates}} {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Dispute templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
